1	package registry
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"fmt"
6		"os"
7		"path/filepath"
8	
9		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/ddxroot"
10	)
11	
12	// GlobalPluginDir returns the filesystem path for a named plugin in the global
13	// tier: ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>.
14	// The directory may not exist; callers that need it present must create it.
15	func GlobalPluginDir(name string) string {
16		return filepath.Join(ddxroot.GlobalDir(), "plugins", name)
17	}
18	
19	// ResolvePlugin returns the on-disk path and the resolution layer for a plugin.
20	// Precedence: project → global → baked-in.
21	//
22	// Layer values:
23	//   - "project": found under <projectRoot>/.ddx/plugins/<name>
24	//   - "global":  found under ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>
25	//   - "baked-in": embedded default; only valid for the "ddx" plugin name
26	//
27	// For baked-in, path is empty — callers should use defaultplugin.FS().
28	// Any other unresolved name returns a non-nil error.
29	func ResolvePlugin(ctx context.Context, projectRoot, name string) (path string, layer string, err error) {
30		projectPluginPath := filepath.Join(ddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot), "plugins", name)
31		if info, statErr := os.Stat(projectPluginPath); statErr == nil && info.IsDir() {
32			return projectPluginPath, "project", nil
33		}
34	
35		globalPluginPath := filepath.Join(ddxroot.GlobalDir(), "plugins", name)
36		if info, statErr := os.Stat(globalPluginPath); statErr == nil && info.IsDir() {
37			return globalPluginPath, "global", nil
38		}
39	
40		if name == "ddx" {
41			return "", "baked-in", nil
42		}
43	
44		return "", "", fmt.Errorf("plugin %q not found in project, global, or baked-in layers", name)
45	}
46

23	func TestInstallPackageHonorsSkillSource(t *testing.T) {
24		projectRoot := t.TempDir()
25		server := newPackageArchiveServer(t, samplePackageTarball(t))
26		defer server.Close()
27	
28		pkg := sampleInstallPackage(server.URL)
29		entry, err := InstallPackage(pkg, projectRoot)
30		require.NoError(t, err)
31	
32		for _, rel := range []string{
33			filepath.Join(".agents", "skills", "ddx", "SKILL.md"),
34			filepath.Join(".claude", "skills", "ddx", "SKILL.md"),
35		} {
36			path := filepath.Join(projectRoot, rel)
37			data, readErr := os.ReadFile(path)
38			require.NoError(t, readErr)
39			assert.Contains(t, string(data), "ddx")
40		}
41	
42		for _, rel := range []string{
43			filepath.Join(".agents", "skills", "ddx", "SKILL.md"),
44			filepath.Join(".claude", "skills", "ddx", "SKILL.md"),
45		} {
46			assert.Contains(t, entry.Files, rel)
47		}
48	}

50	func TestInstallPackagePreservesUnrelatedProjectSkills(t *testing.T) {
51		projectRoot := t.TempDir()
52		server := newPackageArchiveServer(t, samplePackageTarball(t))
53		defer server.Close()
54	
55		for _, rel := range []string{

59			path := filepath.Join(projectRoot, rel)
60			require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(path), 0o755))
61			require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(path, []byte("keep me"), 0o644))

94	func sampleInstallPackage(source string) *Package {
95		return &Package{
96			Name:    "sample-plugin",
97			Version: "1.0.0",
98			Type:    PackageTypePlugin,
99			Source:  source,
100			Install: PackageInstall{
101				Root: &InstallMapping{
102					Source: ".",
103					Target: ".ddx/plugins/sample-plugin",
104				},
105				Skills: []InstallMapping{
106					{Source: "skills/", Target: ".agents/skills/"},
107					{Source: "skills/", Target: ".claude/skills/"},
108				},
109			},
110		}
111	}
112	
113	func samplePackageTarball(t *testing.T) []byte {
114		t.Helper()
115	
116		var buf bytes.Buffer
117		gz := gzip.NewWriter(&buf)
118		tw := tar.NewWriter(gz)
119	
120		topDir := "sample-plugin-1.0.0"
121		writeTarDir(t, tw, topDir)
122		writeTarFile(t, tw, filepath.Join(topDir, "package.yaml"), []byte(`name: sample-plugin
123	version: 1.0.0
124	description: Sample package
125	type: plugin
126	source: https://example.com/sample-plugin
127	api_version: 1
128	install:
129	  root:
130	    source: .
131	    target: .ddx/plugins/sample-plugin
132	  skills:
133	    - source: skills/
134	      target: .agents/skills/
135	    - source: skills/
136	      target: .claude/skills/
137	`))
138		writeTarDir(t, tw, filepath.Join(topDir, "skills"))
139		writeTarDir(t, tw, filepath.Join(topDir, "skills", "ddx"))
140		writeTarFile(t, tw, filepath.Join(topDir, "skills", "ddx", "SKILL.md"), []byte("---\nname: ddx\ndescription: sample\n---\n# ddx\n"))
141	
142		require.NoError(t, tw.Close())
143		require.NoError(t, gz.Close())
144		return buf.Bytes()
145	}
146	
147	func newPackageArchiveServer(t *testing.T, payload []byte) *httptest.Server {
148		t.Helper()
149	
150		return httptest.NewServer(http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
151			if !strings.HasSuffix(r.URL.Path, "/archive/refs/tags/v1.0.0.tar.gz") {
152				t.Errorf("unexpected archive path: %s", r.URL.Path)
153			}
154			w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/gzip")
155			_, _ = w.Write(payload)
156		}))
157	}
158	
159	func writeTarDir(t *testing.T, tw *tar.Writer, name string) {
160		t.Helper()
161	
162		require.NoError(t, tw.WriteHeader(&tar.Header{
163			Name:     filepath.ToSlash(name) + "/",
164			Mode:     0o755,
165			Typeflag: tar.TypeDir,
166		}))
167	}
168	
169	func writeTarFile(t *testing.T, tw *tar.Writer, name string, body []byte) {
170		t.Helper()
171	
172		require.NoError(t, tw.WriteHeader(&tar.Header{
173			Name:     filepath.ToSlash(name),
174			Mode:     0o644,
175			Size:     int64(len(body)),
176			Typeflag: tar.TypeReg,
177		}))
178		_, err := tw.Write(body)
179		require.NoError(t, err)
180	}
181	
182	// samplePackageFS returns an in-memory fs.FS rooted at the package layout that
183	// the remote tarball produces, so both entrypoints exercise the same manifest
184	// and mappings.
185	func samplePackageFS() fstest.MapFS {
186		return fstest.MapFS{
187			"package.yaml": &fstest.MapFile{Data: []byte(`name: sample-plugin
188	version: 1.0.0
189	description: Sample package
190	type: plugin
191	source: https://example.com/sample-plugin
192	api_version: "1"
193	install:
194	  root:
195	    source: .
196	    target: .ddx/plugins/sample-plugin
197	  skills:
198	    - source: skills/
199	      target: .agents/skills/
200	    - source: skills/
201	      target: .claude/skills/
202	`)},
203			"skills/ddx/SKILL.md": &fstest.MapFile{Data: []byte("---\nname: ddx\ndescription: sample\n---\n# ddx\n")},
204		}
205	}
206	
207	// TestInstallPackageFromFSHonorsManifestMappings proves that an embedded
208	// fs.FS rooted at a package directory reads package.yaml and applies both
209	// the root mapping and the declared skill mappings.
210	func TestInstallPackageFromFSHonorsManifestMappings(t *testing.T) {
211		projectRoot := t.TempDir()
212		pkg := sampleInstallPackage("https://example.com/sample-plugin")
213	
214		entry, err := InstallPackageFromFS(pkg, samplePackageFS(), projectRoot)
215		require.NoError(t, err)
216	
217		// Root mapping: package.yaml from the embedded FS lands under .ddx/plugins/<name>/.
218		rootManifest := filepath.Join(projectRoot, ddxroot.DirName, "plugins", "sample-plugin", "package.yaml")
219		_, statErr := os.Stat(rootManifest)
220		require.NoError(t, statErr, "package.yaml should be installed via root mapping")
221	
222		// Skill mappings: each declared target gets the skill directory under it.
223		for _, rel := range []string{
224			filepath.Join(".agents", "skills", "ddx", "SKILL.md"),
225			filepath.Join(".claude", "skills", "ddx", "SKILL.md"),
226		} {
227			path := filepath.Join(projectRoot, rel)
228			data, readErr := os.ReadFile(path)
229			require.NoError(t, readErr, "skill file should exist via manifest skill mapping: %s", rel)
230			assert.Contains(t, string(data), "ddx")
231			assert.Contains(t, entry.Files, rel)
232		}
233	}
234	
235	// TestInstallPackageFromFSInstallsDefaultDDxPackageOffline proves the embedded
236	// default ddx package can install .ddx/plugins/ddx, .agents/skills/ddx, and
237	// .claude/skills/ddx without invoking any network download code.
238	func TestInstallPackageFromFSInstallsDefaultDDxPackageOffline(t *testing.T) {
239		projectRoot := t.TempDir()
240	
241		// Block network: any HTTP attempt should fail this test loudly.
242		origTransport := http.DefaultTransport
243		http.DefaultTransport = &offlineTransport{t: t}
244		t.Cleanup(func() { http.DefaultTransport = origTransport })
245	
246		pkg := &Package{
247			Name:    "ddx",
248			Version: "0.0.0",
249			Type:    PackageTypePlugin,
250			Source:  "https://example.com/ddx",
251		}
252	
253		entry, err := InstallPackageFromFS(pkg, defaultplugin.FS(), projectRoot)
254		require.NoError(t, err)
255	
256		for _, rel := range []string{
257			filepath.Join(ddxroot.DirName, "plugins", "ddx", "package.yaml"),
258			filepath.Join(".agents", "skills", "ddx", "SKILL.md"),
259			filepath.Join(".claude", "skills", "ddx", "SKILL.md"),
260		} {
261			path := filepath.Join(projectRoot, rel)
262			_, statErr := os.Stat(path)
263			require.NoError(t, statErr, "expected embedded default package to install %s", rel)
264		}
265	
266		// The recorded file list must include the skill targets so the install
267		// state can be uninstalled/reverified later.
268		for _, rel := range []string{
269			filepath.Join(".agents", "skills", "ddx", "SKILL.md"),
270			filepath.Join(".claude", "skills", "ddx", "SKILL.md"),
271		} {
272			assert.Contains(t, entry.Files, rel)
273		}
274	}
275	
276	// TestInstallPackageFromRemoteUsesSharedCore proves the remote entrypoint
277	// and embedded-FS entrypoint share the same skill mapping behavior for a
278	// fixture package — same recorded files, same on-disk layout.
279	func TestInstallPackageFromRemoteUsesSharedCore(t *testing.T) {
280		remoteRoot := t.TempDir()
281		fsRoot := t.TempDir()
282	
283		server := newPackageArchiveServer(t, samplePackageTarball(t))
284		defer server.Close()
285	
286		remoteEntry, err := InstallPackageFromRemote(sampleInstallPackage(server.URL), remoteRoot)
287		require.NoError(t, err)
288	
289		fsEntry, err := InstallPackageFromFS(sampleInstallPackage(server.URL), samplePackageFS(), fsRoot)
290		require.NoError(t, err)
291	
292		// Both entrypoints record the same project-relative install paths.
293		remoteFiles := append([]string(nil), remoteEntry.Files...)
294		fsFiles := append([]string(nil), fsEntry.Files...)
295		sort.Strings(remoteFiles)
296		sort.Strings(fsFiles)
297		assert.Equal(t, remoteFiles, fsFiles, "remote and FS entrypoints should record identical install file lists")
298	
299		// And the same skill files actually exist under each project root.
300		for _, rel := range []string{
301			filepath.Join(".agents", "skills", "ddx", "SKILL.md"),
302			filepath.Join(".claude", "skills", "ddx", "SKILL.md"),
303		} {
304			_, err := os.Stat(filepath.Join(remoteRoot, rel))
305			require.NoError(t, err, "remote install missing %s", rel)
306			_, err = os.Stat(filepath.Join(fsRoot, rel))
307			require.NoError(t, err, "fs install missing %s", rel)
308		}
309	}
310	
311	// TestDefaultDDxPackageManifestUsesPackageLocalSkillSource proves the
312	// canonical library/package.yaml declares package-local skill sources
313	// (i.e. source: skills/) so the embedded default-package install copies
314	// the DDx skill out of library/skills/ rather than out of a checked-in
315	// .agents/skills mirror.
316	func TestDefaultDDxPackageManifestUsesPackageLocalSkillSource(t *testing.T) {
317		manifestPath := filepath.Join("..", "..", "..", "library", "package.yaml")
318		pkg, issues, err := LoadPackageManifest(filepath.Dir(manifestPath))
319		require.NoError(t, err, "library/package.yaml must load cleanly")
320		require.Empty(t, issues, "library/package.yaml must have no schema issues")
321		require.NotNil(t, pkg)
322	
323		require.NotEmpty(t, pkg.Install.Skills, "library/package.yaml must declare install.skills mappings")
324		for _, m := range pkg.Install.Skills {
325			assert.Equal(t, "skills/", m.Source,
326				"library/package.yaml install.skills[*].source must be package-local 'skills/', got %q", m.Source)
327		}
328	}
329	
330	// TestDefaultPackageEmbedCopyIncludesDDxSkill proves the embedded
331	// default-package tree used by the binary actually carries the canonical
332	// library/skills/ddx/SKILL.md so offline `ddx init` installs a real skill.
333	func TestDefaultPackageEmbedCopyIncludesDDxSkill(t *testing.T) {
334		data, err := iofs.ReadFile(defaultplugin.FS(), "skills/ddx/SKILL.md")
335		require.NoError(t, err, "embedded default package must include skills/ddx/SKILL.md")
336		require.NotEmpty(t, data, "embedded skills/ddx/SKILL.md must not be empty")
337	
338		canonical, err := os.ReadFile(filepath.Join("..", "..", "..", "library", "skills", "ddx", "SKILL.md"))
339		require.NoError(t, err, "canonical library/skills/ddx/SKILL.md must exist on disk")
340		assert.Equal(t, string(canonical), string(data),
341			"embedded skills/ddx/SKILL.md must match the canonical library/skills/ddx/SKILL.md (run `make copy-skills` to sync)")
342	}
343	
344	// offlineTransport fails any HTTP attempt so tests can prove an install path
345	// did not reach the network.
346	type offlineTransport struct{ t *testing.T }
347	
348	func (o *offlineTransport) RoundTrip(req *http.Request) (*http.Response, error) {
349		o.t.Helper()
350		o.t.Fatalf("unexpected HTTP request during offline install: %s %s", req.Method, req.URL.String())
351		return nil, nil
352	}

data.text: All pre-commit hooks pass. Now commit:

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
registry: add GlobalPluginsDir() and project→global→baked-in plugin lookup precedence [ddx-18e55cd2]

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

data.text:
All ACs satisfied and committed:

- **AC1**: `GlobalPluginsDir()` added to `resolve.go`, returns `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins` with XDG override and `~/.local/share` fallback. `GlobalPluginDir(name)` now delegates to it.
- **AC2**: `TestPluginLookup_PrefersProjectOverGlobal` passes (was already present, verifies `ResolvePlugin` returns project layer when both exist).
- **AC3**: `TestPluginLookup_FallsBackToGlobal` passes (was already present, verifies global and baked-in fallback).
- **AC4**: FEAT-015 rejection tests (`TestInstall_RejectsHomeRootedManifestTarget`, etc.) all pass, `~`-guard untouched.
- **AC5**: `cd cli && go test ./internal/registry/...` green (41 tests).
- **AC6**: `lefthook run pre-commit` passes all 16 hooks.
